name: model-usage
tagline: ClawHub (113★): Use CodexBar CLI local cost usage to summarize per-model usage for Codex or Claude, including the current (most recent) mod
verdict: Main gap: no exclusion guidance, so it's plausible this misfires on adjacent cost/analytics requests it isn't built for. It also skips versioning entirely, and installs via an unpinned brew cask from an individual's third-party tap rather than homebrew-core — not malicious, just less trustworthy than mainstream distribution.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' / exclusion / pointer-to-other-skill text anywhere in description or body; skill is multi-step enough that misfire on adjacent cost/analytics requests is plausible.
No version field in frontmatter, no changelog section, no dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
Install source is an unpinned brew cask from a named individual's third-party tap rather than homebrew-core; this is a standard, non-obfuscated distribution mechanism (no typosquat
